summary: boardflow preinstall downloads and executes remote.exe dropper
details: On `npm install`, the `preinstall` lifecycle hook runs `install.js`, which is heavily obfuscated (obfuscator.io string-array with hex-named variables and rotated/arithmetic-encoded string indices). The script reconstructs the URL `https://www.pooron.org/ice.exe`, downloads the binary to the OS temp directory under a randomized filename (`tester_<hex>.exe`), chmods it 0755, and spawns it detached with `stdio:'ignore'`. Platform branches handle win32, darwin, and linux. The fetched payload is an opaque, unsigned, unpinned executable from a non-publisher domain that has no relationship to the package's stated purpose (a 'lightweight kanban board utility'). The obfuscation has no legitimate purpose in a lifecycle script and exists solely to hide the URL and spawn flow from static scanners. Any machine that runs `npm install boardflow` is compromised by attacker-controlled native code before the developer ever uses the package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
